questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the Essentials Path?

options.text: To teach advanced Notion scripting and API integration
options.explanation: Advanced scripting is not covered; the Essentials Path is for basic skills.

options.text: To help new users gain basic skills to create, collaborate, and organize work efficiently
options.explanation: Correct – the description states the path is for new users to learn core Notion abilities.

options.text: To certify users as Notion consultants
options.explanation: Certification as a consultant is beyond the scope of this beginner path.

options.text: To provide in‑depth training on Notion security settings
options.explanation: Security settings are not mentioned in the Essentials Path overview.

questions.question: Which lesson specifically teaches how to share, collaborate, and brainstorm with team members?

options.explanation: Building basics covers navigation, blocks, databases, and wikis, not sharing.
The lesson titled "Sharing & collaboration" focuses on those topics.
The quiz tests knowledge; it does not teach sharing.
There is no lesson named "Advanced integrations" in this path.

questions.question: How long is the "Building basics" lesson estimated to take?

options.explanation: The article lists 20 minutes, not 10.
The "Building basics" lesson is described as a 20‑minute lesson.
30 minutes is longer than the stated duration.
45 minutes is far beyond the indicated time.

questions.question: Which of the following skills is NOT explicitly mentioned as part of the "Building basics" lesson?

options.explanation: Navigation is listed as a skill covered.
Creating with blocks is explicitly mentioned.
Custom databases are part of the lesson description.
Designing custom themes is not referenced in the lesson overview.

questions.question: What can a learner earn after completing the Notion Essentials Quiz?

options.explanation: The article mentions a badge, not a formal certificate.
The quiz description states you earn a badge to test your knowledge.
No premium features are granted by taking the quiz.
Discounts are not mentioned in relation to the quiz.

questions.question: In which language is the Essentials Path content delivered?

options.explanation: Spanish is not listed; the article shows English.
All lesson tags include "English," indicating the language.
French is not mentioned.
German is also not referenced.

questions.question: If a user wants to learn how to manage knowledge with Wikis, which lesson should they prioritize and why?

options.text: Sharing & collaboration, because Wikis are primarily used for team communication
options.explanation: While collaboration is related, the description of Wikis appears under Building basics.

options.text: Building basics, because the lesson explicitly mentions managing knowledge with Wikis
options.explanation: The Building basics lesson lists "manage knowledge with Wikis" as a learning outcome.

options.text: Notion Essentials Quiz, because quizzes test knowledge about Wikis
options.explanation: The quiz assesses knowledge after learning; it does not teach Wikis.

options.text: None of the lessons cover Wikis; the user should look for an advanced course
options.explanation: Wikis are indeed covered in the Building basics lesson.
